summary.intent: Review PR #653 (feat/deterministic-compaction) for correctness issues, then implement all review findings including clippy blockers, correctness bugs, and test quality improvements.
summary.outcome: All blockers and P2 issues fixed: clippy clean, fmt clean, 201/201 chat lib tests pass (40/40 compaction tests, +1 new). Changes committed and pushed to the feature branch.

repo: The workspace lint config denies both `clippy::unwrap_used` AND `clippy::expect_used` in tests; the pattern to bypass this is a `#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]` attribute on the test module, matching the precedent in crates/chat/src/chat_error.rs.
The nightly toolchain pin is `nightly-2025-11-30`; clippy must be run with `cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 clippy -p moltis-chat --all-targets -- -D warnings`.
The project uses a `beads` issue tracker (not GitHub Issues); issues are created/closed via a `beads` CLI tool.
`mod compaction` is declared privately in lib.rs, so `pub` on functions inside it only exposes them within the crate â€” `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)` is more accurate.
PR reviews are partially automated by a Greptile bot that flags issues with priority levels (P2, etc.) in the PR review state.

code.finding: `compress_summary` bucketed both `<summary>` and `</summary>` as headers, causing them to be placed adjacent at the top with body content outside the tags. Fixed by stripping the wrapper before budget logic and re-wrapping after via a new `split_summary_wrapper` helper.

`collect_unique_tool_names` read the `name` field from ALL message roles, not just tool messages, causing user participant names to appear in the tools list. Fixed by restricting to messages where role is `tool`/`tool_result` via a new `is_tool_role` helper.

`extract_existing_compacted_summary` suffix stripping was fragile: `trim_end_matches(COMPACT_RECENT_MESSAGES_NOTE)` failed because a trailing `\n` was present after the note. Fixed by using explicit `strip_suffix` with `trim_end()` between each step.

`compute_compaction_summary` docstring said it returns `None` for empty input but it actually returned `Some("<summary>â€¦user=0â€¦")`, making the `None` match arm in callers unreachable dead code. Fixed to actually return `None` for empty history.

Fallback budget math computed `notice_len` from `all_dropped` but the final notice used `all_dropped + header_drops`, meaning the notice could exceed budget by 1-2 chars when header drops bumped the count to more digits. Fixed to reserve space for the worst-case count.

The regression test for `compress_summary_protects_summary_tags_under_budget_pressure` only asserted both tags were present, not that they correctly wrapped the body â€” it passed against malformed output. Added `extract_tag_block` assertion to verify body content is actually between the tags.

`extract_existing_compacted_summary_strips_full_wrapping` test used hand-rolled `<summary>...</summary>` tags, but production persists content after `format_compact_summary` converts those to `Summary:\nâ€¦`. Rewritten to build fixture via `get_compact_continuation_message`.
Attribution comment contained a mojibake/zero-width character in the horizontal rule (`â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€ďż˝â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€`). Cosmetic but worth fixing.

workflow: serde_json's `Value` indexing (e.g. `value["key"]`) returns `Value::Null` for missing keys rather than panicking â€” safe to chain without unwrapping, unlike HashMap/Vec indexing.
Running `cargo +nightly clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` before reviewing PR lint status catches test-module lint failures that a targeted `--lib` run might miss.
When fixing fmt issues in a file you're already modifying, it's cleaner to fix them in the same commit rather than leaving pre-existing fmt failures.

friction: Clippy denied `expect_used` in addition to `unwrap_used` in tests, requiring a module-level `#[allow(...)]` attribute â€” this wasn't obvious until the first clippy run after the fix attempt.
Two pre-existing rustfmt failures in test assert formatting blocked `fmt --check` and required additional edit iterations to resolve, separate from the functional changes.

The `compress_summary` tag-reordering bug was masked in production by `format_compact_summary`'s `replacen` call, making it invisible to existing tests and easy to miss without directly inspecting the intermediate compressed string.
open_items: Minor: `collect_unique_tool_names` uses `sort_unstable` + `dedup` which alphabetizes tool names rather than preserving chronological order â€” intentional choice but worth revisiting for UX.
Minor: `compress_summary` case-insensitive dedup (compaction.rs:232-239) can discard distinct bullets that share the same lowercased form â€” unlikely in practice but not addressed.

Minor: `pub` visibility on `summarize_messages`, `merge_compact_summaries`, `format_compact_summary`, `extract_existing_compacted_summary`, `compress_summary` is wider than needed given `mod compaction` is private â€” could be tightened to `pub(crate)` or `pub(super)`.

The `recent_messages_preserved=true` path in `extract_existing_compacted_summary` is now tested but was previously dead code in all production call sites â€” worth tracking whether any caller ever enables this flag.
